source.multipart_basis: BWSC Cruiser Class monocoques are built from male/female molds as bonded CFRP sandwich shell halves with discrete hardpoint inserts and bonded bulkheads. The single-body brief treats the whole tub as one laminate; this multipart decomposition splits it into upper and lower shell halves, front/rear bulkheads, side-impact beams, the main-hoop interface pads, the four seatbelt hardpoint inserts per seat, and the chassis mount inserts. Speculative at the level of ply-schedule partition; all load magnitudes, material properties, and Tsai-Wu / core-shear / mass / modal thresholds are preserved.

full_prompt:
Design the passenger-cell carbon-fiber monocoque for a 2025 Bridgestone World Solar Challenge Cruiser Class vehicle, compliant with international solar-car benchmark 2025 Regulations v1.0. Vehicle gross mass with 2 occupants and battery 500 kg. Solar array area <= 6 m^2. Battery energy <= 11 MJ (3.056 kWh). Monocoque is a carbon/epoxy prepreg sandwich laminate with Nomex honeycomb core; outer skin [45 / 0 / -45 / 90]s laid up as 0.25 mm plies, core thickness 20 mm, inner skin [45 / 0 / -45 / 90]s. Material: carbon/epoxy T700S/epoxy (E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, strength L = 1500 MPa, T = 50 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3); Nomex honeycomb core density 48 kg/m^3, out-of-plane compressive strength 2.4 MPa, out-of-plane shear strength 1.2 MPa. Monocoque bounding 2500 mm (length) x 1600 mm (width) x 1100 mm (height). Integral 2-point anchor pads for roll bar stubs, 4 seat belt anchorages per seat. Crashworthiness / international solar-car benchmark 2025 required load cases: (R1) Rollover: vehicle shall support 1.0 x vehicle mass x g = 1.0 x 500 x 9.81 = 4,905 N applied at the top of the roll bar stubs (the monocoque transfers this into the A-pillar/roof structure); max laminate Tsai-Wu index <= 1.0 and core shear stress <= 1.2 MPa. (R2) Side impact: 3 g lateral applied at seat belt anchor heights on both seats, 3 x 500 x 9.81 = 14,715 N total distributed 50/50 to the two seats; max Tsai-Wu index <= 1.0. (R3) Frontal: 10 g forward applied to the full 500 kg at the front-bulkhead plane, 49,050 N; max Tsai-Wu <= 1.0; peak deceleration at occupant centerline <= 25 g (not a monocoque stress check but a crash simulation output). (R4) Occupant-belt loads per FMVSS 210 analog: 13.5 kN pulled on upper lap belt and 13.5 kN pulled on shoulder belt simultaneously at each seat; max Tsai-Wu at anchor interface <= 1.0. (R5) Monocoque mass <= 50 kg. (R6) First modal frequency hard-mounted at 4 chassis mount points >= 30 Hz. Primary verification: laminate-composite structural FEA (linear static for R1, R2, R4; explicit dynamics recommended for R3). Secondary: modal for R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 outer mold-line + laminate schedule, and a report listing monocoque mass in kg, max Tsai-Wu per load case, core shear stress in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
BWSC Cruiser Class monocoques are manufactured as a bonded assembly of sandwich shell halves with discrete hardpoint inserts; the single-body framing is a ply-schedule simplification, not a manufacturing reality. Speculated families: (A) upper shell half (CFRP sandwich canopy + roof) containing the (J) roll-hoop interface pads; (B) lower shell half (CFRP sandwich floor + side pods) containing the seatbelt hardpoints and chassis-mount inserts; (C) front bulkhead (CFRP sandwich, crash-loaded by LC3); (D) rear bulkhead (CFRP sandwich, closes cell aft of battery); (E) side-impact structure left+right (CFRP beam laminates bonded to B side walls -- primary LC2 path); (F) seatbelt anchor hardpoints (8 total: 4 per seat x 2 seats, Al 7075 or Ti bonded-in insert); (G) chassis mount inserts (4x, Al 7075 bonded-in, support modal LC5); (H) harness/occupant hardpoint reinforcement pads (local ply drop-offs); (J) main roll-hoop interface pad (laminate ramp + titanium insert at A-B joint line, carries LC1). Interfaces: A<->B perimeter bond (continuous structural adhesive); C<->A+B bonded at forward split line (carries LC3); D<->A+B bonded aft; E<->B bonded along sides; F<->B bonded-in inserts at belt anchor points; G<->B bonded-in at chassis-mount positions; H<->B localized ply doublers; J<->A+B roll-hoop interface. FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY per fem_check_policy=assembled_only; per-part Tsai-Wu is reported but the governing check is at the assembled level. Inter-part DCR across bonded interfaces <= 1.0 using adhesive peel+shear allowables. Ply schedule, material properties, load magnitudes, Tsai-Wu<=1.0 thresholds, core shear allowable (1.2 MPa), mass cap (50 kg) and modal floor (30 Hz) are preserved verbatim from the single-body brief.
short_prompt:
Design the passenger-cell carbon-fiber monocoque for a 2025 Bridgestone World Solar Challenge Cruiser Class vehicle as a multi-part bonded assembly, compliant with international solar-car benchmark 2025 Regulations v1.0. Vehicle GVM with 2 occupants and battery 500 kg; solar array area <= 6 m^2; battery energy <= 11 MJ (3.056 kWh).

Carbon/epoxy prepreg sandwich with Nomex honeycomb core: outer skin [45/0/-45/90]s 0.25 mm plies, 20 mm core, inner skin [45/0/-45/90]s. T700S/epoxy (E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, L strength 1500 MPa, T 50 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3). Nomex core density 48 kg/m^3, out-of-plane compressive 2.4 MPa, shear 1.2 MPa. Bounding 2500 x 1600 x 1100 mm. Integral 2-point roll bar anchors; 4 seatbelt anchors per seat.

LC1 rollover 1.0 x mass x g = 4905 N at roll bar stub tops; max Tsai-Wu <= 1.0, core shear <= 1.2 MPa. LC2 side 3 g = 14,715 N at belt anchor heights (50/50 across 2 seats); Tsai-Wu <= 1.0. LC3 frontal 10 g = 49,050 N at front bulkhead; Tsai-Wu <= 1.0; peak occupant decel <= 25 g. LC4 belts per FMVSS 210 analog 13.5 kN lap + 13.5 kN shoulder simultaneously; Tsai-Wu <= 1.0. Mass <= 50 kg. First modal frequency hard-mounted at 4 chassis mounts >= 30 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Frontal 10 g crash sim for occupant-decel (R3) is an explicit dynamics problem.
Tsai-Wu indices are computed per ply post-FEA from element results, not by an FEA solver's built-in von-Mises machinery.

prompt.title: BWSC 2025 Cruiser Class CFRP monocoque (multi-part bonded assembly)
prompt.brief: T700S/epoxy + Nomex honeycomb sandwich passenger cell, 500 kg GVM; passes rollover, side impact, frontal 10 g, belt anchor FMVSS 210 analog. Multi-part: upper shell (A), lower shell (B), front bulkhead (C), rear bulkhead (D), side-impact structures (E x2), belt hardpoints (F x8), chassis mount inserts (G x4), harness reinforcement pads (H), main-hoop interface pad (J). FEM assembled-only; DCR <= 1.0.

material.insert_material: Al 7075-T6 (F, G) or Ti-6Al-4V at J
material.adhesive: structural film adhesive (e.g. Hysol EA 9696), peel 5 kN/m, shear 35 MPa

load_cases.name: side_impact_3g
load_cases.applied_to: E side-impact structures + F belt hardpoints

load_cases.name: belt_loads_FMVSS_210
load_cases.applied_to: F belt hardpoints (4 per seat x 2 seats)

load_cases.name: modal
load_cases.description: hard-mounted at G chassis mount inserts; first modal frequency >= 30 Hz

boundary_conditions.mounting: 4 chassis mount points on G fixed
boundary_conditions.loads: point or distributed at belt anchors F, roll bar stubs J, and front bulkhead C
boundary_conditions.bonded_joints: A<->B, C-to-shells, D-to-shells, E-to-B, inserts F/G/J all modeled as tied surfaces with adhesive check post-process

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B + C + D + E x2 + F x8 + G x4 + H + J)

parts.name: upper_shell_half
parts.role: canopy + roof carrying rollover load path J and upper perimeter of belt anchors F

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP sandwich (outer skin [45/0/-45/90]s, 20 mm Nomex core, inner skin [45/0/-45/90]s)

parts.name: lower_shell_half
parts.role: floor + side pods carrying seatbelt hardpoints, chassis-mount inserts, and side-impact structures; primary LC2 and LC4 path

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP sandwich (same schedule as A) with local ply build-ups under F/G inserts

parts.name: front_bulkhead
parts.role: forward bulkhead that receives the LC3 10 g frontal load and distributes it into A+B shells

geometric_constraints.ply_build_up: doubled skin plies at footwell region (16 plies)
geometric_constraints.crush_mode: progressive skin buckling into honeycomb crush

parts.name: rear_bulkhead
parts.role: rear bulkhead closing the passenger cell aft of the battery

parts.name: side_impact_structure
parts.role: CFRP beam laminates bonded to B side walls; primary LC2 side-impact path

geometric_constraints.construction: solid CFRP laminate or sandwich with local ply build-up

parts.name: seatbelt_anchor_hardpoint
parts.role: bonded-in insert receiving FMVSS 210 analog 13.5 kN belt loads

geometric_constraints.material: Al 7075-T6 bonded into B with film adhesive
geometric_constraints.bolt_interface: M10 lap + M10 shoulder per anchor

parts.name: chassis_mount_insert
parts.role: bonded-in insert carrying the 4-point chassis mount for modal LC5 and ground-handling loads

parts.name: harness_reinforcement_pad
parts.role: local ply doublers where the occupant harness attaches, distributing F insert loads into the surrounding skin

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP additional plies laid up on B inner skin

parts.name: main_hoop_interface_pad
parts.role: laminate ramp + titanium insert at the A-B joint line where the roll-bar stubs anchor; carries LC1

geometric_constraints.ply_build_up: titanium bonded into A+B with local laminate ramp (12+ plies)

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward, Y left-right, Z vertical

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across bonded interfaces (IF1..IF9) <= 1.0 under worst-case load, using adhesive peel and shear allowables (5 kN/m peel, 35 MPa shear).
Insert-to-skin bond (F, G, J) shall not exceed peel or shear allowable under LC1/LC2/LC4.
pass_fail_criteria.metric: max adhesive peel/shear utilization at F/G/J

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: laminated shell (e.g. S4R with composite layup) + solid for inserts F/G/J
connection_modeling: A<->B perimeter bond as tied contact with cohesive zone optional
bonded inserts F/G/J as tied solid-to-shell with adhesive peel+shear post-check
honeycomb core as separate solid or via sandwich-shell formulation

interfaces.mediated_by: continuous perimeter structural adhesive bond (Hysol EA 9696 or equivalent)
interfaces.load_path: all rollover LC1 at J is shared between A and B through this seam

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded with perimeter adhesive and local ply wrap
interfaces.load_path: LC3 10 g frontal load distributed from C into A and B
rear cell closure; minor share of LC3 rebound
LC2 side-impact path into B floor

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded-in Al 7075 insert with film adhesive
interfaces.load_path: LC4 13.5+13.5 kN belt loads into B
chassis mount BC for LC5 modal and ground-handling loads
distributes F insert loads into surrounding skin

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded Ti-6Al-4V insert with local laminate ramp
interfaces.load_path: LC1 rollover 4.9 kN from roll-bar stubs into shells
shear tie between shell perimeter and bulkheads maintains cell integrity under LC2

fem_check_policy.rationale: The monocoque is a bonded assembly; load paths at bonded flanges, discrete inserts, and bulkhead-to-shell junctions can only be correctly resolved with multi-body FEA. Single-body laminate FEA cannot assess the adhesive peel/shear that gates real-world failure.

notes.why_multi_part: Real BWSC monocoques are demolded as upper and lower halves, then bonded with secondary-bonded bulkheads and bonded-in hardpoint inserts; the ply schedule alone does not capture the bonded-interface stress state.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact split-line geometry, ply build-up map, and insert material selection are team-specific. Counts here reflect typical 2-seat Cruiser practice; no additional regulation clauses are cited.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Sandwich panel skins evaluated via Tsai-Wu.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.

fea_experiments.metric: max adhesive peel/shear utilization at F/G/J
